findings.message: Write and Edit declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in any of the 5 command steps; all file operations are delegated to Bash scripts
No formal ## Output section; output format guidance is embedded in step prose rather than a structured template
"better results" is an undefined comparator; does not specify the metric by which vineflower+dex2jar improves output
"better Java output" is an undefined comparator with no metric specified
"best quality" is an undefined comparator
"best results" is an undefined comparator with no metric
"better Java output" in the engine-selection table is an undefined comparator
"better code" is an undefined comparator
"broad sweep" is a vague scope quantifier; does not define which pattern categories are included
cat prints manifest.json from a user-supplied XAPK archive directly to stdout; display-only but content is fully unsanitised
run_grep() captures only $1 as pattern; calls run_grep -i 'regex' pass '-i' as the pattern and silently drop the regex, breaking the --auth case-insensitive search for credentials and base URLs
pkg_install() invokes sudo apt-get/dnf/pacman unconditionally when sudo is available; package names are hardcoded so no injection risk, but sudo elevation proceeds without user confirmation
add_to_profile() permanently appends export PATH and FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH to ~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile without asking the user
install_jadx downloads a zip from GitHub releases via curl/wget with no SHA-256 or signature verification before extracting and making it executable
install_vineflower downloads a JAR from GitHub releases with no integrity check before setting FERNFLOWER_JAR_PATH and executing it
install_dex2jar downloads a zip from GitHub releases with no integrity check before extracting and symlinking scripts into ~/.local/bin
gh_latest_tag() makes an outbound call to api.github.com to resolve the latest release tag; the response is parsed with sed/grep and used directly to construct a download URL without validation
